surface.name: Ory Developer Portal and framework Quickstarts
surface.selection_basis: The Developer Portal is the official documentation home and directs new developers to choose a deployment, product needs, and framework or language before following a Quickstart. It does not select one route across Ory Network, Enterprise License, Open Source, TypeScript, Next.js, Vue, and Go.

alternatives_considered.name: Ory Network
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The portal presents Network, Enterprise License, and Open Source as deployment choices rather than a universal default.

alternatives_considered.name: Next.js App Router Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Next.js is one of several framework peers and its final registration screen actions are not enumerated in the public guide.

alternatives_considered.name: Express.js Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route requires an Ory Network account and project and is not selected over the other framework routes.

entry_point.developer_intent: Add Ory authentication to an application and create the first user.
assumed_prior_state: No Ory account, project, deployment, framework, or package manager has been selected.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is a user created through an Ory-backed registration page, but the documentation home does not select one implementation route and the highlighted Next.js guide does not enumerate the registration form transition.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Not established for a complete public route: the Next.js guide ends by directing the developer to `/auth/registration` to create a user without documenting the required form actions or resulting state.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The portal says `From zero to registered user in minutes`, and the Next.js guide says to create the first user, but the complete transition is not documented.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official portal frames a registered user as the first result, but its deployment and framework choices remain unresolved and the public Quickstart does not provide a reproducible terminal after opening registration.

prerequisites.requirement: Choose an Ory deployment model, product combination, and application framework or language.

primary_path.output: Deployment, product, framework, example, and API-reference starting routes are shown.
primary_path.success_signal: The guided paths and framework choices are visible.

primary_path.action: Resolve the required deployment, product, and framework choices before continuing.
details: Deployment peers are Ory Network, Ory Enterprise License, and Ory Open Source.
The portal currently shows TypeScript, Next.js, Vue, and Go framework choices.
primary_path.output: No universal route is selected by the documentation.
primary_path.success_signal: A human-selected Quickstart would be needed to continue.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation does not provide a default that resolves these choices for an unqualified developer intent.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The documentation home requires peer deployment and framework choices. The visually highlighted Next.js route documents project setup through opening `/auth/registration` but does not enumerate the required registration fields, submission action, or observable created-user result.
candidate_path_gap.impact: No atomic end-to-end candidate can connect documentation arrival to the stated first registered-user terminal without choosing a peer route and inventing its final registration transition.

branches.path: Choose Ory Network, then choose GitHub, Google, or work email on the public account form before creating a free developer project.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an unresolved account-method and project transition before a Network-backed integration.

branches.condition: The developer chooses Next.js App Router.
branches.path: Choose a package manager, create a Next.js app, install `@ory/elements-react` and `@ory/nextjs`, add configuration, middleware, and self-service pages, run the app, and open `/auth/registration`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Reaches the public registration page, but the guide does not document the final form-to-user transition.

friction_gates.description: Deployment, product, framework, and package-manager choices change the required journey and are not globally defaulted.
The intended Next.js first-user terminal requires an undocumented generated registration-form transition.
The Network route presents GitHub, Google, and work-email account methods, whose post-selection convergence is not documented on the public form.

time_to_first_success.value: The Developer Portal says `From zero to registered user in minutes`; no exact duration or complete executable route is supplied for that claim.

sources.title: Ory Documentation | Ory
sections_used: How to use the Ory Developer Portal
How to design your Ory solution

sections_used: Run the application and create your first user

sources.title: Create Your Ory Account: Sign Up & Get Started
evidence_supported: GitHub, Google, and work-email account choices

uncertainties.question: Which deployment model, Ory product set, framework, and package manager define the general first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The choices change account, installation, configuration, and terminal actions.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Developer Portal presents these as contextual peers without one default for the unqualified intent.

uncertainties.question: What exact actions and result create the first user in the Next.js quickstart's generated registration page?
uncertainties.impact: The final required actions and observable success state cannot be recorded.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guide only directs the developer to `/auth/registration` to create a user and does not enumerate the generated form or result.

uncertainties.question: How do the GitHub, Google, and work-email Ory Network account methods converge after their public first screen?
uncertainties.impact: A Network candidate would have unresolved provider, verification, agreement, and account-completion transitions.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public form lists all three methods and the email fields, but does not document the provider screens or post-submit account result.
